sections.label: Section 1150
sections.translation: and avenge my children ’ s blood , or burn my body , which she drove mad , in the fire and so avert from my life the infamy which now awaits me ?

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.original:
ἀλλʼ ἐμποδών μοι θανασίμων βουλευμάτων
Θησεὺς ὅδʼ ἕρπει συγγενὴς φίλος τʼ ἐμός .
sections.translation: But here I see Theseus coming to check my deadly counsels , my kinsman and friend .

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original:
ὀφθησόμεσθα , καὶ τεκνοκτόνον μύσος
ἐς ὄμμαθʼ ἥξει φιλτάτῳ ξένων ἐμῶν .
οἴμοι , τί δράσω ; ποῖ κακῶν ἐρημίαν
εὕρω , πτερωτὸς ἢ κατὰ χθονὸς μολών ;
φέρʼ ⋯ ἄν τι κρατὶ περιβάλω σκότον .
αἰσχύνομαι γὰρ τοῖς δεδραμένοις κακοῖς ·
sections.translation: Now shall I stand revealed , and the dearest of my friends will see the pollution I have incurred by my children ’ s murder . Ah , woe is me ! what am I to do ? Where can I find freedom from my sorrows ? shall I take wings or plunge beneath the earth ? Come , let me veil my head in darkness ;

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original:
καὶ τῶνδε προστρόπαιον αἷμα προσλαβὼν
οὐδὲν κακῶσαι τοὺς ἀναιτίους θέλω .
ἥκω σὺν ἄλλοις , οἳ παρʼ Ἀσωποῦ ῥοὰς
sections.translation: for I am ashamed of the evil I have done , and , since for these I have incurred fresh blood-guiltiness , I do not want to harm the innocent .

sections.label: Section 1163
sections.original:
μένουσιν , ἔνοπλοι γῆς Ἀθηναίων κόροι ,
σῷ παιδί , πρέσβυ , σύμμαχον φέρων δόρυ .
sections.translation: I have come , and others with me , young warriors from the land of Athens , encamped at present by the streams of Asopus ,

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original:
κληδὼν γὰρ ἦλθεν εἰς Ἐρεχθειδῶν πόλιν
ὡς σκῆπτρα χώρας τῆσδʼ ἀναρπάσας Λύκος
ἐς πόλεμον ὑμῖν καὶ μάχην καθίσταται .
τίνων δʼ ἀμοιβὰς ὧν ὑπῆρξεν Ἡρακλῆς
σῴσας με νέρθεν , ἦλθον , εἴ τι δεῖ , γέρον ,
sections.translation: to bring an allied army to your son , old friend . For a rumour reached the city of the Erechtheidae , that Lycus had usurped the scepter of this land and had become your enemy even to battle . Wherefore I came making recompense for the former kindness of Heracles

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.translation: in saving me from the world below , if you have any need of such aid as I or my allies can give , old man .

sections.label: Section 1172
sections.translation: Ha ! why this heap of dead upon the floor ? Surely I have not delayed too long and come too late to check a revolution ? Who slew these children ?

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original:
οὐ γὰρ δορός γε παῖδες ἵστανται πέλας ,
ἀλλʼ ἄλλο πού τι καινὸν εὑρίσκω κακόν .
sections.translation: whose wife is this I see ? Boys do not go to battle ; no , it must be some other strange mischance I here discover .

sections.label: Section 1178
sections.original: ὦ τὸν ἐλαιοφόρον ὄχθον ἔχων ἄναξ ⋯
sections.translation: O king , whose home is that olive-clad hill !

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: τί χρῆμά μʼ οἰκτροῖς ἐκάλεσας προοιμίοις ;
sections.translation: Why this piteous prelude in addressing me ?

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: ἐπάθομεν πάθεα μέλεα πρὸς θεῶν .
sections.translation: The gods have afflicted us with grievous suffering .

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: οἱ παῖδες οἵδε τίνες , ἐφʼ οἷς δακρυρροεῖς ;
sections.translation: Whose are these children , over whom you weep ?

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.original:
ἔτεκε μέν νιν οὑμὸς ἶνις τάλας ,
τεκόμενος δʼ ἔκανε , φόνιον αἷμα τλάς .
sections.translation: My own son ’ s children , woe is him ! he was their father and butcher both , hardening his heart to the bloody deed .

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.original: εὔφημα φώνει .
sections.translation: Hush ! good words only !

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: βουλομένοισιν ἐπαγγέλλῃ .
sections.translation: I would I could obey !

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.original: ὦ δεινὰ λέξας .
sections.translation: What dreadful words !

sections.label: Section 1187
sections.original: οἰχόμεθʼ οἰχόμεθα πτανοί .
sections.translation: Fortune has spread her wings , and we are ruined , ruined .

sections.label: Section 1188
sections.original: τί φῄς ; τί δράσας ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? what has he done ?

sections.label: Section 1189
sections.original: μαινομένῳ πιτύλῳ πλαγχθεὶς
sections.translation: Slain them in a wild fit of frenzy

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: ἑκατογκεφάλου τε βαφαῖς ὕδρας .
sections.translation: with arrows dipped in the venom of the hundred-headed hydra .

sections.label: Section 1191
sections.original: Ἥρας ὅδʼ ἁγών · τίς δʼ ὅδʼ οὑν νεκροῖς , γέρον ;
sections.translation: This is Hera ’ s work ; but who lies there among the dead , old man ?

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original:
ἐμὸς ἐμὸς ὅδε γόνος ὁ πολύπονος , ὃς ἐπὶ
δόρυ γιγαντοφόνον ἦλθεν σὺν θεοῖ-
σι Φλεγραῖον ἐς πεδίον ἀσπιστάς .
sections.translation: My son , my own enduring son , that marched with gods to Phlegra ’ s plain , there to battle with giants and slay them , warrior that he was .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τίς ἀνδρῶν ὧδε δυσδαίμων ἔφυ ;
sections.translation: Ah , ah ! whose fortune was ever so cursed as his ?

sections.label: Section 1196
sections.original:
οὐκ ἂν εἰδείης ἕτερον
πολυμοχθότερον πολυπλαγκτότερόν
τε θνατῶν .
sections.translation: Never will you find another mortal that has suffered more or been driven harder .

sections.label: Section 1198
sections.original: τί γὰρ πέπλοισιν ἄθλιον κρύπτει κάρα ;
sections.translation: Why does he veil his head , poor wretch , in his robe ?

sections.label: Section 1199
sections.original: αἰδόμενος τὸ σὸν ὄμμα
sections.translation: He is ashamed to meet your eye ;

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original:
καὶ φιλίαν ὁμόφυλον
αἷμά τε παιδοφόνον .
sections.translation: his kinsman ’ s kind intent and his children ’ s blood make him abashed .

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἰ συναλγῶν γʼ ἦλθον , ἐκκάλυπτέ νιν .
sections.translation: But I come to sympathize ; uncover him .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον · πάρες ἀπʼ ὀμμάτων
sections.translation: My son , remove that mantle

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original:
πέπλον , ἀπόδικε , ῥέθος ἀελίῳ δεῖξον .
βάρος ἀντίπαλον , δακρύοις συναμιλλαταί ,
ἱκετεύομεν ἀμφὶ γενειάδα καὶ
γόνυ καὶ χέρα σὰν προπίτνων , πολιόν τε
sections.translation: from your eyes , throw it from you , show your face to the sun . As a counterweight , fighting along with my tears , I entreat you as a suppliant , as I grasp your beard , your knees , your hands , and let fall

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original:
δάκρυον ἐκβάλλων · ἰὼ παῖ , κατά-
σχεθε λέοντος ἀγρίου θυμόν , ὡς
βρόμον ἐπὶ φόνιον ἀνόσιον ἐξάγῃ ,
κακὰ θέλων κακοῖς συνάψαι , τέκνον .
sections.translation: the tear from my old eyes . O my child ! restrain your savage lion-like temper , for you are rushing forth on an unholy course of bloodshed , eager to join mischief to mischief , child .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: εἶἑν · σὲ τὸν θάσσοντα δυστήνους ἕδρας
sections.translation: What ! Enough ! To you I call who are huddled there in your misery ,

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original:
αὐδῶ , φίλοισιν ὄμμα δεικνύναι τὸ σόν .
οὐδεὶς σκότος γὰρ ὧδʼ ἔχει μέλαν νέφος ,
ὅστις κακῶν σῶν συμφορὰν κρύψειεν ἄν .
τί μοι προσείων χεῖρα σημαίνεις φόνον ;
sections.translation: show to your friends your face ; for no darkness is black enough to hide your sad mischance . Why do you wave your hand at me , signifying murder ? is it that I may not be polluted by speaking with you ?

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original:
ὡς μὴ μύσος με σῶν βάλῃ προσφθεγμάτων ;
οὐδὲν μέλει μοι σύν γε σοὶ πράσσειν κακῶς ·
καὶ γάρ ποτʼ εὐτύχησα . ἐκεῖσʼ ἀνοιστέον ,
ὅτʼ ἐξέσῳσάς μʼ ἐς φάος νεκρῶν πάρα .
χάριν δὲ γηράσκουσαν ἐχθαίρω φίλων ,
sections.translation: If I share your misfortune , what is that to me ? For once I had good fortune with you . I must refer to the time when you brought me safe from the dead to the light of life . I hate a friend whose gratitude grows old ; one who is ready to enjoy his friends ’ prosperity

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original:
καὶ τῶν καλῶν μὲν ὅστις ἀπολαύειν θέλει ,
συμπλεῖν δὲ τοῖς φίλοισι δυστυχοῦσιν οὔ .
ἀνίστασʼ , ἐκκάλυψον ἄθλιον κάρα ,
βλέψον πρὸς ἡμᾶς . ὅστις εὐγενὴς βροτῶν ,
sections.translation: but unwilling to sail in the same ship with them when they are unfortunate . Arise , unveil your head , poor wretch ! and look on me . The gallant soul endures such blows as heaven deals and does not refuse them .

sections.label: Section 1229
sections.original: φέρει τά γʼ ἐκ θεῶν πτώματʼ οὐδʼ ἀναίνεται .
sections.translation: O Theseus , did you see this struggle with my children ?

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: Θησεῦ , δέδορκας τόνδʼ ἀγῶνʼ ἐμῶν τέκνων ;
sections.translation: I heard of it , and now I see the horrors you mean .

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: ἤκουσα , καὶ βλέποντι σημαίνεις κακά .
sections.translation: Why then have you unveiled my head to the sun ?

sections.label: Section 1232
sections.original: τί δῆτά μου κρᾶτʼ ἀνεκάλυψας ἡλίῳ ;
sections.translation: Why have I ? you , a mortal , can not pollute what is of the gods .

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐ μιαίνεις θνητὸς ὢν τὰ τῶν θεῶν .
sections.translation: Try to escape , luckless wretch , from my unholy taint .

sections.label: Section 1234
sections.original: φεῦγʼ , ὦ ταλαίπωρʼ , ἀνόσιον μίασμʼ ἐμόν .
sections.translation: The avenging fiend does not go forth from friend to friend .

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: οὐδεὶς ἀλάστωρ τοῖς φίλοις ἐκ τῶν φίλων .
sections.translation: For this I thank you ; I do not regret the service I did you .

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: ἐπῄνεσʼ · εὖ δράσας δέ σʼ οὐκ ἀναίνομαι .
sections.translation: While I , for kindness then received , now show my pity for you .

sections.label: Section 1237
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ πάσχων εὖ τότʼ οἰκτίρω σε νῦν .
sections.translation: Ah yes ! I am piteous object , a murderer of my sons .

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: οἰκτρὸς γάρ εἰμι τἄμʼ ἀποκτείνας τέκνα .
sections.translation: I weep for you in your changed fortunes .

sections.label: Section 1239
sections.original: κλαίω χάριν σὴν ἐφʼ ἑτέραισι συμφοραῖς .
sections.translation: Did you ever find another more afflicted ?

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: ηὗρες δέ γʼ ἄλλους ἐν κακοῖσι μείζοσιν ;
sections.translation: Your misfortunes reach from earth to heaven .

sections.label: Section 1241
sections.original: ἅπτῃ κάτωθεν οὐρανοῦ δυσπραξίᾳ .
sections.translation: Therefore I am resolved on death .

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: τοιγὰρ παρεσκευάσμεθʼ ὥστε κατθανεῖν .
sections.translation: Do you suppose the gods attend to your threats ?

sections.label: Section 1243
sections.original: δοκεῖς ἀπειλῶν σῶν μέλειν τι δαίμοσιν ;
sections.translation: The god has been remorseless to me ; so I will be the same to the gods .

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: αὔθαδες ὁ θεός , πρὸς δὲ τοὺς θεοὺς ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Hush ! lest your presumption add to your sufferings .

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original:
ἴσχε στόμʼ , ὡς μὴ μέγα λέγων μεῖζον πάθῃς .
γέμω κακῶν δή , κοὐκέτʼ ἔσθʼ ὅπῃ τεθῇ .
sections.translation: My ship is freighted full with sorrow ; there is no room to stow anything further .

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: δράσεις δὲ δὴ τί ; ποῖ φέρῃ θυμούμενος ;
sections.translation: What will you do ? Where is your fury drifting you ?

sections.label: Section 1247
sections.original: θανών , ὅθενπερ ἦλθον , εἶμι γῆς ὕπο .
sections.translation: I will die and return to that world below from which I have just come .

sections.label: Section 1248
sections.original: εἴρηκας ἐπιτυχόντος ἀνθρώπου λόγους .
sections.translation: Such language is fit for any common fellow .

sections.label: Section 1249
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐκτὸς ὤν γε συμφορᾶς με νουθετεῖς .
sections.translation: Ah ! yours is the advice of one outside sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: ὁ πολλὰ δὴ τλὰς Ἡρακλῆς λέγει τάδε ;
sections.translation: Are these indeed the words of Heracles , the much-enduring ?

sections.label: Section 1251
sections.original: οὐκ οὖν τοσαῦτά γʼ , εἰ μέτρῳ μοχθητέον .
sections.translation: Though never so much as this . Endurance must have a limit .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: εὐεργέτης βροτοῖσι καὶ μέγας φίλος ;
sections.translation: Is this the benefactor and great friend to mortals ?
